scripts.pillar: Ethical AI in spiritual and values-based contexts
scripts.hook: The awkward question layer: what we noticed when AI made belief easier to approach
scripts.draft:
# The awkward question layer: what we noticed when AI made belief easier to approach

Approved by Sean. Full article draft lives in the app default script library / queued article record from commit 1fb0415. Publish to Substack, then repurpose into LinkedIn authority post.
scripts.cta: Publish to Substack, then repurpose into a LinkedIn authority post.

scripts.hook: Trusted communities do not need more apps. They need infrastructure.
scripts.draft: Trusted communities do not need more apps. They need infrastructure. The question isn't whether tech will reshape spiritual practice — it already has. The question is whether it will be built by those who understand what's sacred. [Clarence]
scripts.cta: Where is the biggest infrastructure gap you see in faith communities?

scripts.pillar: Ethical AI in spiritual and values-based contexts
scripts.hook: AI should never replace spiritual authority.
scripts.draft: AI should never replace human authority. But it can support those who hold it — if we build the right boundaries, with the right people in the room. [Clarence]
scripts.cta: What boundaries should hold when AI meets theology?

scripts.hook: Chaplaincy is one of the most human things we do — and one of the least served by technology.
scripts.draft: Chaplaincy is one of the most human things we do — and one of the least served by technology. Spiritual care in hospitals, prisons, and campuses still runs on relationships and paper. What would it look like if the infrastructure actually matched the calling? [Marci]
scripts.cta: What are the biggest gaps in chaplaincy support systems?

scripts.pillar: Ethical AI in spiritual and values-based contexts
scripts.hook: Mindfulness went mainstream. Did it lose its roots?
scripts.draft: Mindfulness went mainstream. But somewhere between the apps and the wellness industry, something got lost. The question isn't whether contemplative practice can scale — it's whether it can scale without losing lineage, context, and accountability. [Marci]
scripts.cta: How do you think about the integrity of tradition as wellness scales?
